title: Modal Dialog
subtitle: Overlay modal dialogs via CDK Overlay with full focus management, animations, minimization, and theme integration

basic.title: Basic Usage
basic.description: Opens a modal from the ModalDialogService. The modal slides in from the edge of the screen with a smooth animation.

semanticTypes.title: Semantic Types
semanticTypes.description: Use semantic helpers to quickly open pre-styled dialogs.
semanticTypes.note: You can launch a state modal with openInfo(), openWarning(), openSuccess(), and openError().

verification.title: Verification Dialog
verification.description: Requires the user to type a specific phrase before confirming a destructive action.
verification.note: Use openVerification() and set verificationText, verificationLabel, and verificationErrorMessage in the configuration to specify the verification text, input field label, and error message displayed when the text does not match.

loader.title: Loading Modal
loader.description: Displays a non-closable loading spinner during long-running operations.
loader.note: Use openLoader() and close the reference when the operation is complete.

dynamicComponent.title: Dynamic Component
dynamicComponent.description: Opens a modal with a custom Angular component as the body.
dynamicComponent.note: Inject MODAL_DIALOG_DATA to receive data and MODAL_DIALOG_REF to control the modal from within.

minimizable.title: Minimizable Modal
minimizable.description: Allows users to minimize the modal to a chip in the bottom dock without losing context.
minimizable.note: A unique id is required when minimizable: true. Calling open() with the same id will restore the minimized modal instead of creating a new one.

timeout.title: Timeout with Progress Bar
timeout.description: Automatically closes the modal after a configurable duration. A visual progress bar tracks the remaining time and can be paused on mouse hover.
timeout.note: Use the timeout option in the modal configuration to enable this feature. You can set duration in seconds, showProgressBar to display the progress bar, pauseOnHover to pause the timer on mouse hover, and timeoutAction to execute a custom function when the time is up ('confirm' | 'cancel').

statusBar.title: Status Bar
statusBar.description: Adds a colored border strip on one side of the modal to convey additional semantic information using the statusBar option in the configuration.

footer-custom.title: Custom Actions
footer-custom.description: Customize the modal actions with custom buttons
footer-custom.note:
You can use the customButtons option in the modal configuration to define buttons with specific actions or nuiModalDialogFooter for full footer customization via an ng-template.

To use loading or disabled reactively, you can inject the ModalDialogActionsService into your child component to control the button states from within the component, use the nuiModalDialogAction directive on your custom buttons to bind them to predefined actions like 'confirm' or 'cancel', or emit custom events and subscribe to them from the child component or using an ng-template, without a directive, from the parent component itself.

child-footer-actions.title: Actions from Child Component
child-footer-actions.description: Customize the modal actions from a dynamic component loaded inside the modal.
child-footer-actions.note: Inject the ModalDialogActionsService into your dynamic component to add buttons to the modal footer from within the component.
child-footer-actions.note2: As an alternative, use the nuiModalDialogFooter directive inside a dynamic component for full control of the footer with native HTML. Unlike ModalDialogActionsService, which registers button data rendered by the modal shell, the directive projects a complete ng-template: any Angular bindings are automatically re-evaluated on each change detection cycle, without needing to call update().

html-content.title: HTML Content
html-content.description: Add HTML content to your message.
html-content.note: Use message for static markup or server-side rendering. The string is sanitized by Angular's DomSanitizer.

body-template.title: Body Template
body-template.description: Pass an Angular TemplateRef via contentTemplate to use Angular bindings inside the modal body. Combine it with templateContext to inject dynamic data.
body-template.note: The template is rendered with *ngTemplateOutlet. Use let-name="key" in the template to access values from the templateContext.

prevent-close.title: Prevent Close
prevent-close.description: Use preventClose to intercept close attempts and block them conditionally. Supports synchronous and asynchronous guards. Subscribe to closePrevented() to react when a close is blocked.
prevent-close.note: The preventClose function must return true (allow) or false / Promise<false> (block). It runs on every close attempt: ESC, backdrop click, and the X button.

backdrop.title: Backdrop and Close Triggers
backdrop.description: Control the visibility of the backdrop and which user actions can close the modal using hasBackdrop, backdropClass, closeOnBackdropClick, and closeOnEscape.
backdrop.note: Setting hasBackdrop: false completely removes the overlay, allowing interaction with the page behind the modal.

update-ref.title: Hot Updates via ModalDialogRef
update-ref.description: Use ref.updateTitle(), ref.updateHeaderTemplate(), and ref.updateFooterTemplate() to update an open modal without closing and reopening it.
update-ref.note: Title updates are reactive via signals and render synchronously, even when called outside the Angular zone (e.g., from setTimeout).

service.title: ModalDialogService
service.description: Inject ModalDialogService to open and manage modals. All methods return a ModalDialogRef.

openDynamic.description: Opens a modal with a dynamic Angular component as the body.

openSimple.description: Opens a simple message or HTML modal.

openVerification.description: Opens a text-verification dialog requiring the user to type a specific phrase.

openLoader.description: Opens a non-closable loading spinner modal. The optional config defaults to canBeClosed: false and closeOnBackdropClick: false.

closeAll.description: Closes all open modals. Accepts an optional reason: 'cancelled' (default) or 'confirmed'.

getById.description: Returns the ModalDialogRef for the modal with the given ID, or undefined if not found.

openPanelsCount.description: Read-only count of currently open modals in the stack.

config.title: ModalDialogConfig
config.description: Configuration object passed to open(). Properties are merged with the global defaults from provideNUI().

title.description: Modal title displayed in the header.

iconTitle.description: Remix Icon class displayed next to the title in the header (e.g. 'ri-alert-line').

message.description: HTML message displayed in the body. Sanitized by Angular's DomSanitizer.

data.description: Data injected into the dynamic component via MODAL_DIALOG_DATA.

width.description: CSS width of the modal panel.

minWidth.description: CSS minimum width of the modal panel.

height.description: CSS height of the modal panel.

minHeight.description: CSS minimum height of the modal panel.

maxWidth.description: CSS maximum width of the modal panel.

modalType.description: Semantic type of the modal: confirm, info, warning, danger, success, or verification.

color.description: NUI color applied to the accent and buttons.

statusBar.description: Colored border stripe displayed on one side of the modal.

canBeClosed.description: When false, hides the close button and blocks all close gestures. Use for loaders.

hasBackdrop.description: Whether a backdrop overlay is rendered behind the modal.

backdropClass.description: One or more CSS classes applied to the backdrop element.

panelClass.description: One or more CSS classes applied to the overlay panel element.

closeOnBackdropClick.description: Whether clicking the backdrop closes the modal.

closeOnEscape.description: Whether pressing the Escape key closes the modal.

closeOnRouteChange.description: Whether the modal closes automatically on route navigation.

preventClose.description: Hook function that runs before every close attempt. Return true to allow or false / Promise<false> to block the close.

autoFocus.description: Auto-focus the first focusable element when the modal opens.

animationDuration.description: Duration of the open and close animations in milliseconds.

scrollStrategy.description: Scroll strategy for the body while the modal is open: 'block' (default) or 'reposition'.

showHeader.description: Whether the default header is visible.

showCloseButton.description: Whether the close button is shown in the header.

hideDefaultHeader.description: Completely hides the header, including the title and close/minimize buttons.

hideDefaultFooter.description: Completely hides the footer, including all action buttons.

headerTemplate.description: Custom TemplateRef that replaces the entire default header.

footerTemplate.description: TemplateRef for the footer. Lower priority than the directive or customButtons.

confirmText.description: Custom label for the confirm button.

cancelText.description: Custom label for the cancel button.

buttonsVariant.description: Default variant applied to the confirm and cancel buttons.

buttonsSize.description: Default size applied to the confirm and cancel buttons.

customButtons.description: Custom footer button definitions with the highest priority.

showDefaultButtons.description: When true, renders the default confirm/cancel buttons alongside customButtons.

isLoading.description: When true, displays a loading spinner in the body.

loadingLabel.description: Text label displayed alongside the loading spinner.

loadingSize.description: NUI size of the loading spinner.

timeout.description: Auto-close countdown configuration with an optional progress bar.

minimizable.description: Whether the modal can be minimized to the dock. Requires a unique id when true.

id.description: Required when minimizable: true. Used as a singleton key to restore a minimized modal.

dockTabConfig.description: Dock chip customization options when the modal is minimized.

contentTemplate.description: TemplateRef for the body. Takes priority over message. Use templateContext to pass context data.

templateContext.description: Context object passed to the body template via *ngTemplateOutlet.

verificationText.description: Text the user must type to confirm a destructive action.

verificationLabel.description: Label displayed above the verification input field.

verificationPlaceholder.description: Placeholder shown inside the verification input field.

verificationErrorMessage.description: Error message displayed when the typed text does not match verificationText.

caseSensitive.description: Whether the verification text comparison is case-sensitive.

ref.title: ModalDialogRef — Methods
ref.description: The reference returned by open(). Use it to control the modal lifecycle and subscribe to events.

close.description: Closes the modal and emits the optional result. Respects preventClose if configured.

minimize.description: Minimizes the modal to the dock. Only has effect if minimizable: true.

restore.description: Restores a minimized modal to the foreground.

canTransitionTo.description: Returns true if transitioning to the given state is valid from the current state.

updateTitle.description: Updates the modal title reactively after it has been opened.

updateHeaderTemplate.description: Replaces the modal header with a custom TemplateRef after opening.

updateFooterTemplate.description: Replaces the modal footer with a custom TemplateRef after opening.

afterOpened.description: Observable that emits once when the modal finishes opening.

afterClosed.description: Observable that emits once when the modal closes, with the optional result.

backdropClick.description: Observable that emits on each backdrop click.

keydownEvents.description: Observable that emits keyboard events from the overlay.

stateChanged.description: Observable that emits on every state transition.

closePrevented.description: Observable that emits when a close attempt is blocked by preventClose.

id.description: Unique identifier of the modal instance.

componentInstance.description: Reference to the dynamically loaded component instance, or null if not yet rendered.

config.description: The resolved configuration object for this modal.

state.description: The current state of the modal (ModalDialogState).

isOpen.description: true if the modal is open or in the process of opening.

isClosed.description: true if the modal is closed or in the process of closing.

isMinimized.description: true if the modal is currently minimized.

stateHistory.description: Read-only array recording the sequence of states the modal has transitioned through.

tokens.title: Injection tokens
tokens.description: Use these tokens to inject modal context inside a dynamic component loaded by the service.

MODAL_DIALOG_DATA.description: Inject the typed data passed via the data config property into the dynamic component.

MODAL_DIALOG_REF.description: Inject the ModalDialogRef instance inside the shell or dynamic component.

MODAL_DIALOG_CONFIG.description: Inject the full, read-only configuration object of the current modal.

customButton.title: ModalDialogCustomButton
customButton.description: Inline button definition passed via customButtons in the config. Evaluated once at open time — use ModalDialogActionsService for reactive states.

color.description: NUI color applied to the button.

variant.description: Button variant: solid, outline, or ghost.

size.description: NUI size applied to the button.

prefixIcon.description: Remix Icon class for the prefix icon.

suffixIcon.description: Remix Icon class for the suffix icon.

callback.description: Click handler invoked with the optional ModalDialogRef. Required.

disabled.description: Static disabled state, evaluated once at open time.

loading.description: Static loading state, evaluated once at open time.

closeOnClick.description: Whether the modal closes automatically after the callback runs.

class.description: One or more additional CSS classes applied to the button element.

modalAction.title: ModalDialogAction
modalAction.description: Footer action registered from a dynamic component via ModalDialogActionsService. Supports reactive disabled and loading via getter functions.

prefixIcon.description: Remix Icon class for the prefix icon.

suffixIcon.description: Remix Icon class for the suffix icon.

color.description: NUI color applied to the button.

variant.description: Button variant: solid, outline, or ghost.

size.description: NUI size applied to the button.

disabled.description: Static boolean or reactive getter — re-evaluated every change detection cycle.

loading.description: Static boolean or reactive getter — re-evaluated every change detection cycle.

loadingPosition.description: Position of the loading spinner relative to the label: start or end.

width.description: Button width mode: auto (content-sized) or full (full container width).

class.description: One or more additional CSS classes applied to the button element.

actionsService.title: ModalDialogActionsService
actionsService.description: Injected by the dynamic component to register footer actions programmatically. A fresh instance is created per modal by ModalDialogService.

register.description: Registers a complete set of footer actions, replacing any previously registered ones.

add.description: Appends a single action to the existing set of footer actions.

update.description: Partially updates the action at the given index.

registerFooterTemplate.description: Registers a TemplateRef as the full footer, overriding actions. Lower priority than the directive.

footerDirective.title: ModalDialogFooterDirective
footerDirective.description: Directive applied to an ng-template inside the dynamic component. Projects the full template into the modal footer with native Angular bindings.

dockTabConfig.title: DockTabConfig
dockTabConfig.description: Customization of the chip shown in the NuiDock when the modal is minimized. Properties are mapped to the NuiDockItem managed by NuiDockService.

label.description: Title override for the dock chip. Falls back to the modal title if not set.

prefixIcon.description: Remix Icon class for the prefix icon displayed inside the dock chip.

suffixIcon.description: Remix Icon class for the suffix icon. Only shown when prefixIcon is set. Useful for indicating additional status such as a notification dot.

hideSuffixIcon.description: When true, hides the directional suffix icon that indicates the original position of a sidebar panel chip.

cssClass.description: One or more additional CSS classes applied to the dock chip button.

colors.title: Color tokens
colors.description: Override these CSS custom properties to adjust modal dialog colors for your brand or custom theme.

layout.title: Layout tokens
layout.description: Control dimensions, spacing, typography, and layer (z-index) of the modal dialog via these CSS variables.

rows.borderRadius: Corner radius of the modal panel.
rows.shadow: Drop shadow of the modal panel.
rows.zIndex: Stack layer for the overlay backdrop.

dock.title: NuiDock tokens
dock.description: Customize the appearance of the unified dock for minimized items (modals and sidebar panels). The --nui-dock-* tokens control the chips; use --nui-dock-z-index and --nui-dock-offset for positioning.

rows.zIndex: Stack layer for the dock toolbar.
rows.maxWidth: Maximum width of the dock container.
rows.separatorColor: Separator color between modal and sidebar chip groups.

keys.title: Translation tokens
keys.description: The Modal Dialog component uses its own tokens and some shared tokens.
keys.note: All user-facing text in Modal Dialog can be overridden globally using provideNuiI18n(). Others can be translated by passing the custom translation in the open() configuration.

rows.close: Aria label and title of the header close button.
rows.minimize: Aria label and title of the header minimize button.
rows.restore: Label for the dock restore chip.
rows.modalDialogVerificationLabel: Label of the verification text field.
rows.modalDialogVerificationPlaceholder: Placeholder of the verification text field.
rows.modalDialogVerificationErrorMessage: Error message shown when the verification text does not match.

roles.title: ARIA roles and attributes
roles.description: The modal shell automatically applies the required ARIA attributes for screen reader compatibility.

rows.role: Identifies the element as a dialog.
rows.ariaModal: Informs assistive technologies that content outside the modal is inert.
rows.ariaLabelledby: Associates the heading with the dialog. Applied automatically when a title is set.
rows.ariaDescribedby: Associates the body region with the dialog.

focus.title: Focus management
focus.description: The CDK FocusTrap ensures keyboard navigation is confined to the modal while it is open.
focus.note: Focus is automatically restored to the element that triggered the modal when it closes.

rows.focusTrap: Keyboard focus is contained within the modal while it is open (Angular CDK FocusTrap).
rows.focusRestoration: When the modal closes, focus returns to the element that triggered it.
rows.escapeKey: Pressing Escape closes the modal. Configurable via closeOnEscape.
rows.autoFocus: When true (default), the first focusable element receives focus on open.

options.title: Global config options
options.description: Default values applied to every modal opened with ModalDialogService. Override per-modal by passing config to open().

rows.hasBackdrop: Show a backdrop overlay behind the modal by default.
rows.closeOnBackdropClick: Close the modal on backdrop click by default.
rows.closeOnEscape: Close the modal on Escape key press by default.
rows.closeOnRouteChange: Auto-close the modal on route navigation by default.
rows.showCloseButton: Show the header close button by default.
rows.autoFocus: Focus the first focusable element on open by default.
rows.buttonsVariant: Default button variant for confirm and cancel actions.
rows.buttonsSize: Default button size for confirm and cancel actions.

example.title: Usage example
example.description: Configure global defaults in your app.config.ts using provideNUI().
